It is a reset button yes
Her 3 cost is weird because its pretty meh without strength but no one else in retaliate cares about str really
You use it on a character or wheel and you have the option of resetting them to level 1 or fully dumping them
Why would you do that? Does it refund your materials?
Yes
When you reset a character you get all the materials you spent plus a standard selector for each enlighten it removes
Because it removes those too
And you can turn shitty character dupes into good character dupes

OK. So if you invested into a character early on and you don't like them, you can basically undo it.
Do they hand these items out often?
If you reset someone who was E3 for example you'd get 3 selectors if you  dump the character entirely you get +1 selector for dumping the character itself
Nope
These were just compensation ones
Use them before their expiry date wisely

If I may ask, is there specific rules for Exalt effect triggers? 
For example if I Equip a wheel that boost crit chance AFTER exalt trigger, will the owner Exalt benefits from buff?
Meanwhile i ate shit brcwuse of the single-turn hp loss cap

That cuts short the turns you boost a lot with temporary power.

And trying to go around it left the boss long enough to do extra "your entire health+300" attacks...

Stuff in the game is applied in order it triggers: and when it affects cards its _often_ left to right in hand.


The exalt stuff that says "after exalt"  after exalt doesn't affect the exalt directly iirc when I tested; but stuff that says a character gets something in exalt does(like 24's awaken card)
The "in the order as it triggers" is important for some interactions

If you use something that increases card cost, then something that reduces the cost to 0, the card will always cost zero.


But doing the reverse, the card will cost 2.

Seen with stuff like hymellyn's +2 cost from awaken and ramona exalt.

Or the "sluggish" debuff and ryker's cost reduction
The order you do stuff that defines which effect applies last/on top
That boss in particular semi skips the first... 4 or so turns? So Tide of Stealth is less huge.

Also hymellym and i assume other exalts seems to affect itself during the exalt itself/as it multi hits.

Like, if first hit applied vul, the others get extra damage even before exalt is over
And i would assume the strenght gain on hit is the same

Well, the last effect is top effect - that's kinda obvious. 
I though "after exalt triggers" works the same way as Sagna Exalt, it's supposed to gain shield first, supposedly after trigger, and only then deal damage with dmg benefit from shield gained
It was still  enough of a problem for me to just use a cognition cause I was genuinely running out of ways to not eat multiple death resistance triggers on the 2nd attack

In regards to cost: Reducing a card's cost to 0 seems to be done "Apply - X to this card's cost, where X is the card's current cost"

Which is important since the game actually does track excess cost reduction.
For example of you have a 4 cost card and reduce it's cost by 5, then activate Hamelin's +2 cost card, it'll become a 1 cost card from 0.

It is something to remember cause this game, unlike other card games ; do the effects in order and "at the same level of priority".

The cost-is-0 from Ramona being only the reduction of the current cost  is different from some other games where the cost would remain zero even if you applied + 2 after the fact

Yee; exactly that 

You can also let cards " over prep" (with those "reduce cost by 1 at end of turn" ) so they are still 0 even when using hynellyn's +2
Yep.
It threw me off hard cause the way the gpt translation words it I assumed her exalt would work like MTG's "costs 0 this turn" or the slay the spire cost nullification
Inversely, if you use Hamelin's card that makes things cost 2 less, use Ramona ult, and then play any other card, that card you Ramona ulted will become a 2 cost card.
Wow, didn't expect these games exist, when effect on card works like a rule that cannot be broken

Talents tend to guide what you end up stacking Wheel/Covenant wise, I feel.
Especially on supportive characters.
Depends on the character
Especially when combined with covenants or wheels that also key off the stat.
Like Sangas doesn't really matter that much, but Lily hard scales with death res
For example, Ramona using Thais Wheel, Chapter 7 Covenant, and her passive may as well stack as much Silver Key Charge as possible.
Even if the stat has diminishing returns if everything she's using is keying off it you may as well.
Though other characters care a bit less, I.e. A decent number of characters with Black Sigil talents don't really need much of that stat.
